creator: jonaspalm@posteo.de
text:
We have the opendkim socket configured in /usr/local/etc/mail/opendkim.conf:
Socket                  local:/var/tmp/dkim.sock

/usr/local/etc/rc.d/milter-opendkim states:
#  all parameters below now can be set in opendkim.conf(5)

but if I try to restart it the following happens:

# /usr/local/etc/rc.d/milter-opendkim restart
Stopping milteropendkim.
Waiting for PIDS: 31334.
Starting milteropendkim.
usage: chmod [-fhv] [-R [-H | -L | -P]] mode file ...

I tracked the chmod error with a few echos to dkim_start_postcmd(). While
[ -S ${milteropendkim_socket##local:} ]
evaluates to true, because $milteropendkim_socket is empty and '[ -S ]' seems to evaluate to true, the
chmod ${_piddir_perms} ${milteropendkim_socket##local:}
afterwards fails with obvious reasons.

Workaround was to set milteropendkim_socket in rc.conf
